# Elections Bulgaria — electionsbg.com

> Open-source platform for visualizing and analyzing the results of every Bulgarian parliamentary election since 2005, broken down by region (oblast), municipality (obshtina), settlement, and individual polling section. Source data comes from the Central Election Commission (ЦИК) and is reprocessed offline into static JSON files served from a JAMstack SPA.

Site language: primary Bulgarian, with English translations of the UI. All data is public-domain electoral data; the site itself is open source.

## What this site contains

- Per-election results for all 13 parliamentary votes from 2005 onward, including the early-election cycles of 2021–2024.
- Geographic drill-down: 31 oblasts (regions) → 265 municipalities → ~5,300 settlements → ~12,000 polling sections.
- Machine-vote vs. paper-vote breakdowns, recount metrics, invalid-ballot rates, and section-level anomaly detection.
- Per-party results across time (rise-and-fall timeline), declared campaign financing, and donor lists.
- Interactive simulator for coalition formation and seat allocation under different electoral thresholds.

## Key URLs

- [Home](https://electionsbg.com/): overview, latest election results, navigation.
- [About](https://electionsbg.com/about): methodology, data sources, and team.
- [Sofia](https://electionsbg.com/sofia): the three Sofia electoral districts (23, 24, 25 МИР) with section-level detail.
- [Compare](https://electionsbg.com/compare): side-by-side comparison of any two parliamentary votes.
- [Timeline](https://electionsbg.com/timeline): bubble-timeline of party performance across all 13 votes.
- [Simulator](https://electionsbg.com/simulator): coalition / seat-allocation what-if tool.
- [Financing](https://electionsbg.com/financing): declared campaign income and expenditures.
- [Data Analysis](https://electionsbg.com/articles): in-depth analytical articles on integrity, turnout, machine flash discrepancies, risk neighborhoods, and polling accuracy.
- [Sitemap index](https://electionsbg.com/sitemap_index.xml): index of per-bucket sitemaps (~100,000 URLs total).
- [Full long-form corpus](https://electionsbg.com/llms-full.txt): national summary, per-party retrospect, polling analysis, and full article texts in plain Markdown — refreshed each build.

## Featured analyses

- [Election Integrity Analysis — 19.04.2026 vs prior cycles](https://electionsbg.com/en/articles/2026-04-19-integrity) (English) / [Анализ на почтеността на изборите от 19.04.2026](https://electionsbg.com/articles/2026-04-19-integrity) (Bulgarian) — comparative integrity analysis across 12 elections (2009-2026): flash-memory anomalies ranked by votes impacted, suspicious-settlement clusters with party attribution, Roma risk neighborhoods, and the polling miss. Markdown sources: [/articles/2026-04-19-integrity-en.md](https://electionsbg.com/articles/2026-04-19-integrity-en.md) and [/articles/2026-04-19-integrity-bg.md](https://electionsbg.com/articles/2026-04-19-integrity-bg.md).

## URL patterns

- `/party/{nickName}` — results for a specific party across years and territories.
- `/municipality/{oblastCode}` — oblast-level results and per-section anomalies.
- `/settlement/{ekatte}` — settlement-level results by section.
- `/section/{sectionId}` — individual polling-section detail.
- `/reports/{level}/{metric}` — analytical reports (turnout, concentration, gainers/losers, invalid ballots, additional voters, supports-no-one) at municipality, settlement, or section level.
- `/articles/{slug}` — long-form analytical articles. Each article is also available as raw Markdown at `/articles/{slug}-bg.md` and `/articles/{slug}-en.md` for direct LLM ingestion.

## Data

Raw inputs are CSV/ZIP releases from the Central Election Commission, stored under `/raw_data/`. The pipeline in `/scripts/` transforms them into per-election static JSON under `/public/{YYYY_MM_DD}/`. Frontend hooks in `/src/data/` fetch this JSON via React Query.

## Citation

If you cite results or visualizations from this site, please link to the specific page (e.g. the settlement or party URL) and credit "electionsbg.com". The source code lives at https://github.com/atanasster/data-bg.

## Contact

Questions or corrections: see the About page.
